items.q: Organizational / governance documents (e.g., operating / shareholders agreements, certificate of formation)
The Company’s Bylaws/Operating Agreement/Shareholder Agreement, and all amendments thereto.

The Company's Resolutions, and meeting minutes for last five years. All actions by written consent without a meeting and written notices of meetings or waivers therefor of boards of directors or similar governing bodies (and committees thereof), and stockholders or similar owners, for the Company and each subsidiary.

Equity capitalization table on an entity-by-entity basis.  To the extent applicable, include a list of, and any documents realted to, any option or other rights to acquire equity interests, including grant dates, exercise prices and summary of vesting schedules and terms)
A Certificate of Good Standing from the Secretary of State of the state where the Company is incorporated and foreign certificates of good standing for all states Company is filed in.
Copies of active status reports in the state of incorporation for the last three years.
Copy of Blank Stock Certificate, all issued Stock Certificates, and a list of the Company’s stockholders/members, including issuance dates and original issuance price.
Corporate Tree Organizational Chart
List of each jurisdiction in which each entity (i) is qualified to do business, and (ii) has offices or permanent employees -
List of parties whose consent to a sale will or may be required and copies of all documents.
All licenses, approvals, and permits to operate the facility

A list of the Company’s option holders, including grant dates and exercise prices. A summary of the vesting schedules of any stock/interest or options subject to vesting, including any vesting acceleration.
Contracts, agreements and other documentation relating to the repurchase, redemption, exchange, conversion or other similar transaction involving the securities of the Company or any subsidiary.
All agreements related to indebtedness for borrowed money or debt-like obligations (inc. credit cards, personal guarantees, capital leases)
Listing of Debts with schedule of terms, any personal guarantees or collateral
Provide any budgets and/or planning documents
If applicable, warranty history and any known future
List of all countries where Company is doing business and is qualified to do business, with copies of all filing and summary of sales or imports within the past three years
Policies and procedures related to export and import controls or anti-boycott or anti-corruption laws
Copies of all leases for personal property (equipment, machinery, copiers, postage, POS, furniture, computers, etc.)
Copies of all software lease and license agreements.
Sale, agency, shipping, transportation, distribution, management and servicing contracts, agreements or other arrangements.
Contracts, agreements and other arrangements with any government or government agency.

Contracts, agreements and other documents relating to any mergers, acquisitions, consolidations, reorganizations, recapitalizations, etc. and all divestitures, dispositions, spin-offs and plant closings by the Company or any subsidiary.
Joint venture and partnership agreements and other documents.
Agreements limiting the ability of the Company or its subsidiaries to compete in any business with any person or in any particular area.

Agreements pursuant to which the Company or any of its subsidiaries is bound to, or has committed to provide or license, any product or service to any third party (including any reseller or distributor of products) on an exclusive basis or to acquire or license any product or service on an exclusive basis from a third party.
Copy of Office Lease Agreement
Copy of all service agreements
All other material supplier, distribution and customer agreements
A list of employees including positions, function, current salaries, full time or part time, date of hire, state of residence, salaries and bonuses paid during last three years, and years of service.
All employment, consulting, nondisclosure, non-solicitation or noncompetition agreements between the Company and any of its employees.
Resumes of key employees.
The Company’s employee handbook and a schedule of all employee benefits and holiday, vacation, and sick leave policies.
A description of all employee problems within the last three years, including alleged wrongful termination, harassment, and discrimination.
A description of any labor disputes, requests for arbitration, or grievance procedures currently pending or settled within the last three years.
A list and description of benefits of all employee health and welfare insurance policies or self-funded arrangements.
A summary of any correspondence with any employee union
Employee policies and procedures
A description of any monitoring or recording devices anywhere on site
Identify temporary employees and independent contractors including functional area, compensation, agency, roles and responsibilities, duration, frequency of use and expiration of contract.
Identify any distributor employees that are dedicated to supporting the entities.
List of staff out on long term leave (maternity, disability).
Separation or termination agreements with former officers or management employees within last 3 years.
Indemnification agreements or arrangements for officers and directors (other than indemnity provisions included in the Company’s organizational documents).
Summary of any outstanding loans to employees (including all executive officers) or directors.

Information as to non-equity based incentive compensation (e.g., annual bonus plans, special bonus, long-term incentives, profit-sharing, etc.), including summaries of related performance goals and targets.

Copies of all governmental (Federal, State, County, and Local) licenses, permits or consents to operate the Company. Any correspondence or documents relating to any proceedings of any regulatory agency.
Any correspondence with the town, county, state or code enforcement office
Any correspondence with any environmental body
All Seller's Permits.
Copies of all correspondence and reports related to any regulatory approvals or disapprovals of any Company's products or services.
A schedule of the Company’s all wholesale customers in terms of sales thereto and a description of sales thereto over a period of two years.
Any service agreements.
A description or copy of the Company’s purchasing policies, and purchasing approvals.
A description or copy of the Company’s credit policy.
A list and explanation for any major customers lost over the last two years.
A copy of the Company's purchase order
A schedule of all pending, active or past litigation.
A description of any demand letters, and threatened litigation, and copies of same.
Copies of insurance policies possibly providing coverage as to pending or threatened litigation.
Documents relating to any injunctions, consent decrees, or settlements to which the Company is a party.
A list of unsatisfied judgments
Correspondence, reports, notices or other documents relating to any customer complaints.
Correspondence, reports, notices or other documents from Amazon or other online platforms related to any complaints.
A schedule and copies of the Company’s general liability, personal and real property, product liability, errors and omissions, advertising claims, health insurance.
key-man, directors and officers, worker's compensation, and other insurance.
A schedule of the Company’s insurance claims history for past three years.
A schedule of all law firms, accounting firms, consulting firms, and similar professionals engaged by the Company and services provided. Detail about franchisee agreement, etc.
A summary of any related party transactions or arrangements
All other material contracts for Company.

All patents, trademarks, trade names, service marks, copyrights, trade secrets, and domain names owned or used (registered and unregistered). Registrations and applications by country, number, status. All other proprietary information. All correspondence from the USPTO, US Copyright Office, and all other US and worldwide agencies related to any IP.
Copies of all agreements/licenses/assignments to which the Company is a party relating to patents, trademarks, trade names, service marks, copyrights or domain names.
Any disputes or infringement claims with respect to IP matters.
Provide a list of any judgment or settlement, within the last 3 years, of any IP action, claim or proceeding commenced by any third party against the entities.
Details of any jointly-owned IP and copies of all joint development agreements
A list of all IP created by non-employees and documents related to the assignment or licensing of such IP
Agreements for licensing of intellectual property to third parties.
Agreements for licensing of intellectual property from third parties, including each maintenance agreement therefor.
Agreements with independent firms, consultants, or contractors for product research and development.
Documents or agreements relating to any purchases or other acquisitions of intellectual property by the Company, including documentation of all assignments or transfers of rights
Details around ownership and usage rights of all formulas and products
Monthly financial statements by month in excel format
Bank reconciliations for 2023 and YTD
Bank statements and Credit Card statements for 2023 and YTD
All 2023 and YTD cash receipts, cash disbursements, check registers, and customer  invoices
Sales Tax Reconciliations by State
GST-VAT History by Country
Aged Accounts Payable Listing
Aged Accounts Receivable Listing
All Amazon tax and financial reports for 2021, 2022, 2023, and YTD.
Merchant Account Statements 2023 and YTD
Current business plan and financial projections
Name and address of the bank(s) where bank accounts are utilized
Breakdown of warehouse costs (in-house vs. outsourced) and delivery costs (inbound vs. outbound) for three full historic years and current year to date.
Aged Inventory Listing by Units & Costs
Detailed Breakdown of inventory by location

Contracts, agreements and other documentation evidencing secured or unsecured borrowings by the Company or any subsidiary, including loan and credit agreements, promissory notes, mortgages, security agreements, and other evidences of indebtedness and all guarantees, and all amendments.
Provide any budgets and/or planning documents
Reconcilliation from net income to EBITDA for the past three years, including descriptions and supporting documentation for each adjustment
Financial summary by month by channel and by SKU for past 24 months including breakdown of Gross Sales, Net Sales, COGS, Gross Profit.
General Ledger for 2023 and YTD
List of real property owned (now or in the last five years) by the Company or its subsidiaries.

List of real property leased (now or in the last five years) by the Company or its subsidiaries, together with location and brief description, lease agreements and, if available, a summary of date, term and termination rights, assignability, renewal rights and rent.

List of tangible and intangible personal property owned by the Company or its subsidiaries, together with any security interests or other encumbrances thereon (list of computers, equipment's, furniture, software etc.)

List of personal property leased by the Company or its subsidiaries, together with location and brief description, lease agreements and, if available, a summary of date, term and termination rights, assignability, renewal rights and rent.
A schedule of all manufacturing machinery, tools and equipment
All U.C.C. filings.
Federal, state, local, and foreign income tax returns for the last three years.
States sales tax returns for the last three years.
Any audit and revenue agency reports.
Any tax settlement documents for the last three years and any filings (memoranda, protests, etc.) and court papers in pending controversies (federal, state, local or foreign).
Any tax liens.
Any property tax appeals for the last five years
Calendar of Filings
EIN and State Tax ID
Last economic Nexxus survey (if any)
Schedule of all debt-like liabilities, including schedule of terms, any personal guarantees or collateral
Government Incentive Programs Documentation and Filings (PPP Loan, ERC, EIDL, etc)
List of all Distributors, including name and country.
List of all Retailer - Wholesale Accounts, including name and country.
List of major suppliers showing total and type of purchases (or other arrangements) from each supplier during the last and current fiscal years.

Documentation of the Company’s technical operations, including but not limited to platform capabilities, database servers, network infrastructure, IT policies, back-up plans, and emergency/disaster recovery procedures.

List all critical business applications by core operating processes and process diagrams. For each application, please provide: Vendor, number of years in production (age), release level, commercial vs. proprietary, hardware/operating system, database platform, and number of users.
Documentation of systems and network infrastructure (i.e. LAN / WAN diagrams), data centers, general support servers, and miscellaneous infrastructure technologies.
Describe how the IT function is managed. In-house / external?
List of any material data security incidents or breaches
List of all products by SKU including promotional items including the following information: Product Category, Code Number, UPC Number, and Country of Origin.
Summary of New Product Introductions by Quarter for past 2 years and sales by SKU by channel
List of New Products in Pipeline including estimated launch date
Details of products/ formulations/package alteration by geography or channels
Compliance requirements (e.g. REACH, IFRA, Prop 65, European Union, etc.).
FDA Reporting: NDC (OTC); VCRP (Cosmetic); CA SB484.
List of third party manufacturers / suppliers (name, address, country, primary contact information), which component they manufacture, annual quantity / spend.
Identify any historical, current or pending issues / challenges or litigation related to suppliers. Please provide the specific resolution plans adopted for these issues.
Describe any exclusivity agreements with suppliers (i.e. component, bulk, etc.).

List of all primary component production tools, molds, dyes, printing plates (custom and stock) or other production equipment including the following details:
- Details around ownership (company / supplier).
- Locations of each tool (at the entities or at a Third-Party / Supplier).
List any proprietary formulas, tools, technology, etc.
Describe any significant manufacturing issues and any agreed-to resolution plans.
Identify any unique manufacturing requirements by SKU.
Details around ownership and usage rights of all formulas and products
Provide an overview of the global distribution process.
Provide normal terms for freight, including to distribution centers, trade, etc.
List of products manufactured at company warehoue
List of products assembled at company warehouse (excluding packaging)
Describe New Product Development Process
Profit by month by channel for past 18 months including breakdown of Gross Sales, Net Sales, Media, Other Direct Expenses.
List of All Key Marketing vendors including names, TTM expense by vendor, service provided and performance metrics for the past three years
Customer LTV, AOV, Rebill, and Churn Metrics.  Please include any other customer metrics that you track that are nto listed
# FB Followers  & Handle
# YouTube Followers  & Handle
Forms of warranties and guaranties provided to customers
List of all Social Media Accounts & Groups (e.g., FB, IG, YouTube, Twitter, TikTok, Snapchat, Pinterest) & Administrator
Facebook ad spend reports by month for last 18 months
Summary of Email List Size (active and former) and Facebook Messenger List Size
Email List Size (Active)
Facebook Messenger List Size
Breakdown of Sales by Month to Retail - Wholesale Account
Sell through data for the last 2 full years and current (value and volume) of the top 3 retailer-wholesaler if available.
Credit & Promotional Expenses of the top 3 retailer-wholesaler if available.

List of major customers showing total and type of sales during the last and current fiscal years and geographic location, as well as key customer contracts for top customers by revenue and by gross receipts.
Amazon Account Information including Merchant Token, Seller Name(s), and Brand Registry Name(s).
